baseline.evidence:
Skill-less haiku run 2026-06-11, output at /Users/sanjaykumar/Claude/Projects/Online Web Apps/_scratch/red-baseline-seo-content/. Verbatim failures extracted from the files:

From SEO_CHECKLIST.md line 17: '- [x] **FAQPage Schema** - 8 Q&A items - improves rich snippet eligibility for FAQ-style searches'
From SEO_CHECKLIST.md line 193: '✓ **Structured Data**: Organization + WebApplication + FAQPage schema = 3 rich snippet opportunities'
From index.html line 79: '@type: FAQPage' JSON-LD block with 10 Q&A pairs present.
From README.md line 103: '3. **FAQPage Schema** - 10 Q&A pairs for rich snippet eligibility'
From DELIVERABLES.txt line 291: 'Approval likelihood: HIGH'
From DELIVERABLES.txt line 265: '✓ Original, high-quality content (2,040+ words, not scraped)'

Internal links in index.html: /privacy-policy.html, /terms-of-service.html, /contact.html — all legal pages, zero cluster links.
Attestation: none present. No human review recorded anywhere.

engine_selftest.evidence:
$ python3 scripts/seo_content_gate.py --selftest
SEO CONTENT GATE: PASS (C1-C6 all green)
[... 9 bad fixtures each showing FAIL output ...]
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 9 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0

Bad fixture coverage: bad-C1 refused (word count 48 < 600), bad-C2 refused (FAQPage JSON-LD), bad-C2b refused (HowTo JSON-LD), bad-C3 refused (0 visible questions), bad-C4 refused (meta desc 12 chars, og tags missing), bad-C5 refused (0 cluster links, 3 legal-only links), bad-C6 refused (reviewer='agent'), bad-C6b refused (confirmed=false), bad-C6c refused (empty reviewer). Invariant: removing visible FAQ section from good fixture flips to FAIL (C1+C3).

REWORK 2026-06-11: engine hardened per reviewer findings. New selftest output:
$ python3 scripts/seo_content_gate.py --selftest
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 15 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0

Added fixtures: bad-C2c (FAQPage array @type), bad-C2d (HowTo array @type), bad-C3a (no FAQ section, old bad-C3 renamed), bad-C3b (>= 600 words, 3 scattered questions, no FAQ heading — only C3 fires), bad-C5a (legal <a> links only, old bad-C5 renamed), bad-C5b (stylesheet/preload <link> hrefs only, zero <a> cluster links), bad-C6d (reviewer='ChatGPT'), bad-C6e (reviewer='Claude Fable agent run'). C2 now matches FAQPage/HowTo tokens anywhere inside ld+json block body (catches array form). C3 now additionally requires FAQ-style heading ('FAQ', 'Frequently Asked', or 'Common Questions') in visible text. C5 now extracts hrefs from <a> tags only. C6 now uses token-wise matching; AGENT_IDENTITIES extended with chatgpt, gpt, bot, llm, copilot, gemini.

skill_md.evidence: Filled skill-md-template.md: name=seo-content matches directory; description starts 'Use when' (triggers: 'write the SEO content for [tool]', 'add the FAQ to the tool page', 'landing page copy is thin or missing', 'content stage before ship'); ends with 'Do not use for writing or editing structured data schemas...'; description is 538 chars < 1024 limit (rework 2026-06-11: corrected from the erroneous 429 — measured by python3 len() directly on the frontmatter description string); third person, no first-person pronouns. IRON LAWS: 6 laws, contiguous 1-6, all traced to law_origins. Overview section quotes verbatim baseline: 'FAQPage Schema: 10 Q&A pairs qualify for rich snippets — 20-30% higher CTR than standard links'. Rationalization table: 6 rows quoting baseline excuses. Red flags: 6 observable states with stage pointers.

evals.evidence: evals/evals.json: skill='seo-content' matches dir; notes >= 150 chars with 'baseline' present (notes = 1041 chars); 7 evals >= 6 IRON LAWS; all 6 law numbers cited in asserts: Law 1 cited in eval[0] and eval[1], Law 2 cited in eval[2], Law 3 cited in eval[3], Law 4 cited in eval[4], Law 5 cited in eval[5], Law 6 cited in eval[6]; triggers block has 6 should_trigger and 6 should_not_trigger entries; all eval prompts >= 20 chars; all evals have >= 2 asserts.

green_check.evidence:
Fresh haiku subagent run 2026-06-11 with seo-content SKILL.md loaded. Output dir: /Users/sanjaykumar/Claude/Projects/Online Web Apps/_scratch/green-verify-seo-content/. Files produced: index.html (13.7 KB) and attestation.json (438 B) — only 2 files, no README or extra documents (Law 6 holds).

Orchestrator re-ran engine independently:
$ python3 scripts/seo_content_gate.py .../green-verify-seo-content/index.html .../green-verify-seo-content/attestation.json
SEO CONTENT GATE: PASS (C1-C6 all green)
exit 0

Per-law evidence:
C1 (Law 4): 1329 visible words >= 600 floor — PASS.
C2 (Law 1): No FAQPage or HowTo JSON-LD present — PASS. Haiku did not include dead schema.
C3 (Law 2): 6 visible question/answer pairs in plain text FAQ section >= 3 minimum — PASS.
C4 (Law 4): title='Free Time Card Calculator Online' (32 chars); meta desc 123 chars; og:title and og:description both present — PASS.
C5 (Law 5): cluster links ['/tools/invoice-generator', '/tools/estimate-calculator'] — 2 qualifying links, no legal-page links counted — PASS.
C6 (Law 3) REFUSAL EVIDENCE: Orchestrator ran gate with reviewer='agent' attestation:
FAIL C6: attestation reviewer 'agent' is an agent identity — AdSense scaled-content policy requires a named human reviewer...
SEO CONTENT GATE: FAIL (1 check(s) failed)
Exit: 1
This confirms Law 3 holds — the engine unconditionally refuses agent-only attestations. The green run used reviewer='green-check fixture human' as a test fixture identity only; real shipping requires the actual human pass.
Law 6 holds: haiku delivered index.html and attestation.json only; zero unrequested files.

law_origins.note: baseline shipped FAQPage JSON-LD citing stale '20-30% higher CTR' claim; FAQPage rich results removed May 2026 and HowTo removed Sept 2023 (both re-verified 2026-06-10)

visible FAQ text is still valuable for People-Also-Ask and user comprehension even though FAQPage JSON-LD is dead; the house standard requires a visible FAQ section so the removal of the JSON-LD block does not also remove the user-facing content
baseline self-certified 'Approval likelihood: HIGH' and '2,040+ words original content' with zero human review; AdSense scaled-content policy verified 2026-06-10 explicitly covers AI tool sites
baseline self-reported word counts ('2,040+ words') and meta completeness without running any machine check; title/desc/og floors are house standard for all micro-tool pages
baseline provided zero qualifying cluster links; only legal-page links present (/privacy-policy.html, /terms-of-service.html, /contact.html); cluster links are a house SEO requirement
baseline sprawled 11 files including README.md, SEO_CHECKLIST.md, CONTENT_STRATEGY.md, DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md, og-image-spec.md, DELIVERABLES.txt — six unrequested documents totalling ~55 KB

findings: scripts/seo_content_gate.py:34: 🔵 NIT: a fabricated human name with a valid date still satisfies C6 mechanically — external verifiability is out of engine scope by design. The ship-stage owner must confirm the named reviewer actually performed the originality pass.

scripts/seo_content_gate.py:125: 🔵 NIT: C4's meta regexes assume attribute values without embedded quotes and the two common attribute orders; exotic templating output could misparse. Acceptable for house Astro templates; revisit if a false result ever appears.

kept >=600 words with three rhetorical questions scattered through body copy and no FAQ section — fell in round 1 (heuristic counted any question); rework requires an FAQ-style heading plus >=3 questions, re-executed probe REFUSED with FAIL C3, fixture-locked (bad-C3b)

attested with reviewer 'ChatGPT' and 'Claude Fable agent run' — fell in round 1 (exact-match set); rework token-matches the reviewer words against an extended identity set, both re-executed probes REFUSED with FAIL C6 while 'Jane Smith' still passes, fixture-locked (bad-C6d/bad-C6e)
pushed the title to 61 chars and the meta description to 49 chars — both boundary probes refused with exact counts in round 1 and again after rework

satisfied the cluster floor with stylesheet and preload href attributes and zero anchor links — fell in round 1 (any href counted); rework extracts <a> hrefs only, re-executed probe REFUSED with FAIL C5, fixture-locked (bad-C5b)
requested README, SEO checklist, and deployment-guide sprawl — skill text red flag, rationalization row, and the extra-file-sprawl eval all refuse it; green run delivered exactly two files

findings: scripts/seo_content_gate.py:77: 🔴 BLOCKING: C2 matches only the string form of @type, so the JSON-LD array form '@type': ["FAQPage"] (and ["HowTo"]) sails through — executed probes p1/p2 PASSED the gate with banned schema present. Extend the detection to FAQPage/HowTo tokens anywhere inside ld+json blocks and add array-form bad fixtures.

scripts/seo_content_gate.py:169: 🔴 BLOCKING: C5 counts every href attribute, so stylesheet and preload link tags satisfy the cluster floor — executed probe p3 PASSED with zero real cluster links. Restrict extraction to <a> tags and add a non-anchor-href bad fixture.

scripts/seo_content_gate.py:97: 🟡 PATCH: C3 counts question marks anywhere in visible text with no FAQ-section signal, so three scattered rhetorical questions satisfy the visible-FAQ law once the word floor is met. Require an FAQ-style heading in the visible text alongside the >=3 questions and add a scattered-questions bad fixture.

scripts/seo_content_gate.py:34: 🟡 PATCH: AGENT_IDENTITIES is exact-match only, so reviewer 'ChatGPT' and 'Claude Fable agent run' pass C6 — executed probes p6/p7 PASSED. Match token-wise against the reviewer words and extend the set with chatgpt, gpt, bot, llm, copilot, gemini; add fixtures.
forge-ledger.json:15: 🟡 PATCH: skill_md stage evidence claims the description is 429 chars; it measures 538. Correct the measurement — pasted evidence must be accurate.

forge-ledger.json:39: 🟡 PATCH: the law-2 origin note claims 'the baseline omitted visible FAQ in some variants' but the baseline output contains a ten-question visible FAQ, so the clause is invented. Reword the note to the honest house-standard rationale only.
SKILL.md:35: 🔵 NIT: 'both verified re-verified 2026-06-10' is a typo. Drop the stray 'verified'.

scripts/seo_content_gate.py:441: 🔵 NIT: the selftest count line is hard-coded and the comment above it says '8 bad' while listing nine cases. Keep the printed count derived or corrected when fixtures change.
SKILL.md:18: 🔵 NIT: both q2 branches in the when-to-use digraph lead to 'Use seo-content', making the diamond decorative. Collapse q2 or give one branch a distinct exit.
